meta.title: Japan Grid & Data-Center Power Suppliers
meta.subtitle: Vetted Japanese manufacturers to import, represent, or broker in the US
meta.note: Verify all contact details and sales-inquiry routes through each company's official website before outreach; personnel, regional responsibilities, and inquiry forms change without notice.

suppliers.name: Hitachi Energy Ltd.
suppliers.nameJp: 日立エナジー株式会社
suppliers.category: Grid: Transformers, HV switchgear, HVDC & grid automation
suppliers.products: Power transformers (all voltage classes), gas-insulated switchgear (GIS up to 1,200 kV AC / 1,100 kV DC), HVDC converter stations, STATCOM/SVC, protection IEDs, substation automation (MicroSCADA X), grid management software (Network Manager EMS/ADMS); EconiQ SF6-free switchgear line; power semiconductors (IGBTs, IGCTs, thyristors).
suppliers.hq: Zurich, Switzerland (subsidiary of Hitachi Ltd., Tokyo, Japan)
suppliers.exportNote: Hitachi Energy has US manufacturing and engineering offices. Nine Japanese T&D utilities selected Hitachi for a next-generation nationwide load-dispatching system; strong US utility references via SunZia HVDC and grid-automation contracts.
suppliers.relevance: World-leading grid-equipment OEM descended from ABB Power Grids, majority-owned by Hitachi Japan; a tier-1 source for HV T&D hardware and grid-automation software for US utilities seeking Japanese-engineered, globally serviced technology.

suppliers.name: Mitsubishi Electric Corporation
suppliers.category: Grid & Data-center power: Transformers, HV switchgear, HVDC, UPS & critical power
suppliers.products: Generator step-up and autotransformers, HV/MV gas-insulated switchgear, HVDC systems, protection and supervisory systems, energy management and network control systems; UPS systems for data centers (9900CX/9900D/SUMMIT Series, 300–2,100 kVA), containerised Critical Power PODs, MV/LV data-center switchgear, backup generators (OEM-partnered).
suppliers.exportNote: Mitsubishi Electric Power Products Inc. (MEPPI) in Pittsburgh, PA is the dedicated US subsidiary covering UPS, transformers, and switchgear for North America; SUMMIT Series UPS holds the #1 Energy Star rating in its AC double-conversion category.
suppliers.relevance: Dual-category giant covering grid T&D hardware and mission-critical data-center UPS; MEPPI already operates in the US—ideal for identifying product lines or geographic segments where additional representation or brokering is needed alongside the existing sales channel.

sources.name: Mitsubishi Electric US
sources.title: Data Center UPS Solutions — United States

suppliers.name: Toshiba Energy Systems & Solutions Corporation
suppliers.nameJp: 東芝エネルギーシステムズ株式会社
suppliers.category: Grid: Gas-insulated switchgear, power transformers, HVDC & substations
suppliers.products: SF6 and natural-origin gas GIS (72–500 kV; AEROXIA brand), oil-immersed and totally gas-insulated power transformers (including the world's only fully SF6-gas-cooled large-capacity transformers), HVDC VSC converter stations, polymer-housed surge arresters, substation automation compliant with IEC 61850; micro-grid energy management systems.
suppliers.exportNote: Toshiba ESS has delivered GIS and transformers to Iraq, UAE (Fujairah F3 CCPP 400 kV), Singapore, Pakistan, and Maldives; active internationally and a credible supplier for US utility high-voltage substation projects.
suppliers.relevance: One of Japan's premier GIS and power-transformer makers with a 135-year heritage; sole manufacturer of fully SF6-gas-insulated and gas-cooled large-capacity transformers — a technically differentiated product for US utilities pursuing SF6 phase-out mandates.

suppliers.name: Fuji Electric Co., Ltd.
suppliers.category: Grid & Data-center power: Transformers, GIS, switchgear, UPS & total data-center systems
suppliers.products: Oil-immersed and cast-resin transformers (100 V–168 kV), GIS (72–300 kV), gas circuit breakers, SVC/STATCOM, grid-scale lithium-ion battery storage; UPS systems (Japan's #1 market share) from 30 kVA to 2,400 kVA per unit (7500WX Series with 98.5% efficiency, SiC-equipped); modular SKID and container-type data-center power packages; in-house turbine and backup generators.
suppliers.exportNote: Fuji Electric Corp. of America (FECOA) has operated in the US since 1970. Fuji developed a North America-specific SiC-equipped UPS (UPS7300WX-T3U) and exhibited total data-center systems at Data Centre World Asia 2025 under the concept 'New AI era with Grid × Green.'
suppliers.relevance: Unique dual-coverage: Japan's largest UPS supplier AND a full-range grid-equipment maker offering genuine turnkey data-center electrical systems — a one-stop pitch rare outside the hyperscale tier; strong for US data-center developers and co-location operators.

suppliers.name: Meidensha Corporation
suppliers.category: Grid: HV switchgear, transformers, protection relays, surge arresters & mobile substations
suppliers.products: High-voltage vacuum circuit breakers and GIS, power and distribution transformers, mobile substations, all-digital protection relays (MEDIMUL multi-function and MRR unit-type series, JEC2500 compliant), gapless zinc-oxide surge arresters (SORESTER), photovoltaic and hydroelectric power systems; co-developed AEROXIA natural-origin-gas GIS with Toshiba.
suppliers.exportNote: Meiden Singapore is one of Southeast Asia's largest switchgear and transformer suppliers, with 30,000+ switchgear panels delivered; Tridelta Meidensha JV exports surge arresters to 120+ countries including North America.
suppliers.relevance: Established mid-tier OEM with a deep export track record in switchgear, transformers, and protection relays; surge arresters and digital protection IEDs are in high demand for US grid-modernisation and wildfire-resilience programmes — clear representation opportunity.

suppliers.name: DAIHEN Corporation
suppliers.category: Grid: Power transformers, substation switchgear & power distribution equipment
suppliers.products: Oil-immersed (Top Runner standard) and cast-resin power transformers, shunt reactors, power-receiving and substation switchgear (circuit breakers, cutout fuses, pole switches), distribution panels and remote monitoring systems, distributed power supply equipment, solar inverters; also industrial robots and welding machines.
suppliers.exportNote: Founded 1919 as Osaka Transformer Co.; OTC DAIHEN USA (welding/robotics arm) has been in the US since 1979 with headquarters in Ohio. The power-products division (transformers, switchgear) is far less visible in North America — a direct brokering opportunity.
suppliers.relevance: Century-old transformer and switchgear OEM with 30+ global subsidiaries; its power-equipment arm is largely unknown in the US compared to its welding/robotics division — a prime opening for a US import or distribution agreement on the power side.

suppliers.name: Takaoka Toko Co., Ltd.
suppliers.nameJp: 高岡トーコー株式会社
suppliers.category: Grid: Transformers, switchgear, IEDs & smart-grid automation (DMS/SCADA/microgrid)
suppliers.products: Extra-high-voltage to pole-mounted and pad-mounted transformers, gas voltage transformers for GIS, medium- and low-voltage metal-enclosed switchgear (power centers, motor-control centers), protection IEDs, distribution management systems (DMS/@Station), SCADA (@SCADA+), microgrid controllers (@MicroGrid), synchrophasor wide-area monitoring (SmartWAMS), solar-plant control and fault-location systems.
suppliers.exportNote: 35% owned by TEPCO Power Grid; group offices in Vietnam and Philippines; gas voltage transformers already exported to GIS manufacturers in Asia, Middle East, and Europe. Company leadership has explicitly stated ambition to reach global users.
suppliers.relevance: Covers the entire voltage stack from EHV substations to pole-mounted distribution plus integrated smart-grid software — and has signalled a clear export ambition; an ideal partner for a US broker targeting investor-owned utilities and rural co-ops.

sources.name: The Worldfolio
sources.title: Takaoka Toko: The Power Behind Change — Executive Interview

suppliers.name: Nissin Electric Co., Ltd.
suppliers.category: Grid: Instrument transformers, HV power capacitors & automated distribution systems
suppliers.products: Current transformers, voltage transformers, capacitor voltage transformers, SF6-free gas-insulated voltage transformers (up to 170 kV ECO Gas type), HV power capacitors and filters, automated power distribution systems (33 kV, delivered internationally), micro-substation systems for off-grid/developing regions; ion-implant and beam equipment (separate division).
suppliers.exportNote: Sumitomo Group member; 24+ subsidiaries in Japan, China, Taiwan, Korea, Thailand, Vietnam, India, USA, and Spain. Delivered 15 units of 33 kV automated distribution system to YESC (Yemen); developed Japan's first EMS for automatic self-consignment PV operation.
suppliers.relevance: Niche leader in instrument transformers and HV capacitors — components that US utilities replace at scale during grid upgrades; the Sumitomo Group link provides financial stability and a potential introduction channel via Sumitomo Electric Industries USA.

sources.name: Sumitomo Electric
sources.title: Nissin Electric Co., Ltd. — Group Company Profile

suppliers.name: Hitachi Industrial Equipment Systems Co., Ltd.
suppliers.nameJp: 日立産機システム株式会社
suppliers.category: Grid & Data-center power: Grid-forming inverters, drives & grid-edge solutions
suppliers.products: Grid Forming Inverters (GFM) for microgrid stabilisation and renewable-energy integration (commercially operational at Narashino Works, 2025); variable-speed drives (AC/DC); solar/battery/fuel-cell power conditioners; AC microgrid and DC microgrid systems; compressed-air and fluid systems; data-center power and cooling efficiency products; serves data centers, batteries, electronics, and semiconductor manufacturing.
suppliers.exportNote: Part of Hitachi Group; launched Japan's first commercial grid-forming inverter installation in April 2025 with a Ministry of Economy carbon-reduction subsidy — technology directly relevant to US renewable-integration grid codes (IEEE 2800).
suppliers.relevance: Emerging leader in grid-forming inverter technology (a high-demand segment as US ISO/RTOs mandate synthetic inertia); simultaneously serves data centers — a dual-revenue opportunity for a US technical representative.

sources.name: Hitachi Group News
sources.title: Hitachi IES Launches Next-Generation Grid Forming Inverter System (April 2025)

suppliers.name: Toshiba Mitsubishi-Electric Industrial Systems Corporation (TMEIC)
suppliers.nameJp: 東芝三菱電機産業システム株式会社
suppliers.category: Grid & industrial: MV power electronics, drives, FACTS & power conversion systems
suppliers.products: Medium-voltage variable-speed drives (TMdrive-MVG series, 3–11 kV), large industrial power converters, static var compensators (SVC), FACTS devices, solar and wind power conversion systems, motor-control systems for steel mills, cement, and mining; also relevant for data-center UPS and power-quality applications.
suppliers.exportNote: TMEIC Corporation (US subsidiary) is based in Roanoke, Virginia; however, specific MV-drive and FACTS product lines for niche industrial and utility segments may benefit from additional regional representation.
suppliers.relevance: 50:50 Toshiba–Mitsubishi Electric JV producing industry-leading MV drives and FACTS equipment used in US grid and heavy-industrial applications; suitable for brokering to US power-quality integrators, renewable-energy developers, and industrial-utility customers.

sources.name: ensun
sources.title: Top Switchgear Manufacturers in Japan — TMEIC Listed

suppliers.name: Sansha Electric Manufacturing Co., Ltd.
suppliers.nameJp: 三社電機製作所株式会社
suppliers.products: Online UPS systems (1 kVA to large capacity; lithium-ion battery option; track record with Ministry of Defense, JAXA, major hospitals, banks, and data centers), grid-connected inverters for solar/storage/fuel cells, thyristors, triacs, diodes, SiC MOSFET power modules (2-in-1 SiC MOSFET); industrial power regulators and rectifiers.
suppliers.exportNote: SanRex Corporation, Sansha's wholly owned US subsidiary since 1982, is based in Port Washington, New York, confirming an established North American distribution and service framework.
suppliers.relevance: Niche Japanese UPS and SiC-power-module maker with a proven government/defense track record (JAXA, Ministry of Defense) and a US subsidiary already in place; smaller data-center operators and federal/defense integrators are underserved — strong brokering opportunity.

sources.name: SanRex Corporation
sources.title: About SanRex — Sansha Electric US Subsidiary (Port Washington, NY)

suppliers.name: Togami Electric Mfg. Co., Ltd.
suppliers.nameJp: 戸上電機製作所株式会社
suppliers.category: Grid: Distribution switchgear, ring main units, reclosers & electromagnetic contactors
suppliers.products: Ring Main Units (RMU) for ring-network protection and control, automatic reclosers, HV/MV distribution switchgear and switchboards, LV power-distribution boards, electromagnetic contactors and starters (standard, DC-operated, explosion-proof, corrosion-resistant), motor control centers; underground fault locators and line-tracing instruments.
suppliers.exportNote: TSE-listed (6643); International Division actively develops MV products up to 38 kV for overseas markets across Southeast Asia, Middle East, and selected Western markets. No dedicated US entity — clear representation gap.
suppliers.relevance: 100-year-old specialist in distribution switchgear, RMUs, and reclosers; these products are in high demand for US rural-utility, co-op, and microgrid projects; limited direct US presence makes an exclusive or preferred-rep arrangement straightforward to negotiate.

sources.name: Japan IR
sources.title: Togami Electric Mfg Co — TSE:6643 Company Profile

sources.name: JMAG International
sources.title: Togami Electric — International Division Interview (MV Export Focus)

suppliers.name: Terasaki Electric Co., Ltd.
suppliers.nameJp: 寺崎電気産業株式会社
suppliers.category: Grid & Data-center power: Circuit breakers, MV switchgear & motor control centers
suppliers.products: Low-voltage and medium-voltage air circuit breakers (TemBreak2/TemPower2 ACB), moulded-case circuit breakers, earth-leakage and multi-protection relays (TemTrip2, TZS), HV metal-clad switchgear (HL22), arc-protection switchgear (APS22), generator and marine power-distribution switchgear (MS22/GS22/GS50), AC motor control centers (CL21); DC circuit breakers; monitoring and control systems (TERANET 50X, WE500) for data-center and marine applications.
suppliers.exportNote: First company in Japan to develop circuit breakers entirely in-house; global sales through Terasaki Electric Europe (UK, Italy, Spain) and agents in 30+ countries. No dedicated US subsidiary — confirmed open gap in the North American market.
suppliers.relevance: World-class Japanese circuit-breaker and LV/MV switchgear brand with IEC-compliant products proven in marine, data-center, and utility sectors globally; the absence of a US entity is a direct opening for an exclusive import or distribution arrangement.

sources.name: DirectIndustry
sources.title: Terasaki Electric Circuit Breaker — Full Product Catalog

suppliers.name: Sanyo Denki Co., Ltd.
suppliers.category: Data-center power / UPS & precision power systems
suppliers.products: Three-phase online UPS (SANUPS series for data centers, telecoms, financial institutions, and broadcasting), single-phase UPS, DC power systems, high-reliability server-rack cooling fans and blowers; also AC servo systems and stepping motors for precision applications.
suppliers.exportNote: JEMA member; Sanyo Denki has regional offices and authorised distributors across North America, Europe, and Asia. The UPS product line has lower US brand recognition than Mitsubishi Electric or Fuji Electric — significant white-space for a targeted regional rep.
suppliers.relevance: Established Japanese UPS and precision power-systems maker with a strong quality reputation in Asia-Pacific; smaller scale and lower US profile make brokering or regional distribution agreements more accessible than approaching the major OEMs directly.

sources.name: JEMA
sources.title: Japan Electrical Manufacturers' Association — Member Directory (Sanyo Denki listed)
